_meta.description: Maps revealed form sections to the variable_weights keys they inform. Used to evaluate whether information obtained from a revealed section was actually used in the agent's decision (via non-zero variable weight). Designed for urological biopsy decision-making following EAU/ESUR mpMRI-guided protocols.
notes: primary_variables: the variable_weights keys whose source data lives exclusively or primarily in this section.
secondary_variables: keys that can be corroborated or enriched by this section but are primarily sourced elsewhere.

always_available_variables: 'psa' (baseline) and 'age' are visible in the patient card before any section is revealed; a section reveal is not required to weight them, though richer context (density, trend) requires sections.
section_s3-psa is present in the HTML form but absent from the README section table; it is included here as it appears in real reveal sequences.

always_available_variables.description: Variables derivable from the patient card (patient.psa_ng_ml, patient.age_years) that are visible before any section is revealed. Agents may weight these without revealing any section.

clinical_rationale.pirads: The mpMRI report is the sole source of the PI-RADS score (1-5), lesion location, size, and zonal classification. PI-RADS is the cardinal variable for risk stratification; it cannot be assigned without this section.
clinical_rationale.vol: Prostate volume is routinely measured from mpMRI (planimetry or ellipsoid formula on T2 sequences). This is the reference volume used to calculate PSA density; the agent cannot compute PSAD without it.

clinical_rationale.psad: PSA density (PSA / prostate volume) is displayed as a calculated field in the laboratory panel. While the component values come from PSA (labs or patient card) and volume (MRI), the computed PSAD value is presented here.
clinical_rationale.dre: Digital rectal examination findings (Normal / Benign / Nodus / Suspicious / Abnormal) are documented alongside laboratory values in this form's clinical workup panel. DRE is the primary clinical palpation finding and lives here.
clinical_rationale.cspca: The clinically-significant prostate cancer probability (e.g., Stockholm3 or ERSPC risk calculator output) is shown as a computed risk score in the laboratory results alongside PSA and other biomarkers.
clinical_rationale.psa: The current PSA measurement (ng/mL) is typically listed in the laboratory panel. Note: the raw PSA value is also visible in the patient card; this section adds precision, flags, and reference ranges.
clinical_rationale.vol: Some centres report TRUS-derived prostate volume in the laboratory/radiology workup section. Where TRUS volume is listed here it may supplement or replace the MRI-derived volume.

clinical_rationale.psa: This section provides the longitudinal PSA trajectory — serial measurements, PSA velocity (ng/mL/year), and PSA doubling time. Kinetic data materially changes how heavily PSA is weighted relative to a single static value.
clinical_rationale.psad: Where volume has been stable across follow-up visits, PSAD velocity can be inferred from the PSA trend. This section may show how PSA density has evolved, reinforcing or weakening its weight.

clinical_rationale.bx: The pathology report is the definitive source for prior biopsy outcome: ISUP grade group, Gleason pattern, number of positive cores, percentage core involvement, and laterality. Without this section the agent has no basis for weighting prior biopsy status.

clinical_rationale.fh: Family history of prostate cancer in first-degree relatives (father, brother) is documented exclusively in this section. Per EAU guidelines a positive family history shifts the PSAD threshold and influences the biopsy decision at PI-RADS 2-3.

clinical_rationale.bx: Previous clinical encounter notes frequently contain the most detailed narrative of prior biopsy decisions, outcomes, and follow-up plans — including cases where the formal pathology report is sparse. For re-biopsy scenarios this section is critical.
clinical_rationale.psa: Earlier PSA values recorded in prior visit notes can supplement or extend the PSA trend timeline beyond what the dedicated PSA trend section shows.
clinical_rationale.dre: Prior DRE findings documented in previous notes provide longitudinal context (e.g., progressive induration) that increases the clinical weight of a currently suspicious DRE.
clinical_rationale.fh: Family history information may be mentioned in previous notes when the patient disclosed it at an earlier visit, even if the dedicated family history section was not formally updated.

clinical_rationale.comorbidity: This section documents active medical conditions (cardiovascular disease, diabetes, frailty index, ASA class), current medications, and life-expectancy estimates. Comorbidity is the override variable: severe comorbidity with life expectancy < 10 years shifts the decision to watchful waiting regardless of imaging findings.
clinical_rationale.age: While age is visible in the patient card, this section provides functional age context — frailty, ECOG status, and multi-morbidity — that determines whether chronological age translates into a meaningful contraindication to biopsy.

pirads.note: PI-RADS score is available only from the MRI report. If the agent weights pirads above 'not_used', it should have revealed section_s3-mri.

psad.note: PSAD is computed from PSA (labs) and prostate volume (MRI). The calculated PSAD value is displayed in the labs section. Both section_s3-labs and section_s3-mri provide the components; section_s3-labs shows the final computed value.

psa.note: Baseline PSA is visible in the patient card; no section reveal is strictly required. However, PSA velocity and trend data require section_s3-psa or section_s3-labs. Weight above 'noted' implies the agent used more than the bare patient-card value.

dre.note: DRE findings are displayed in the laboratory/clinical workup panel. Previous notes may contain historical DRE findings for longitudinal context.

cspca.note: The csPCa probability (risk model output) is shown as a calculated value in the laboratory results panel.

age.note: Age is always visible in the patient card. No section reveal is required. section_s3-comorb adds functional age context (frailty, comorbidity burden).

fh.note: Family history of PCa in first-degree relatives is documented in section_s3-fh. It may also appear in previous clinical notes.

vol.note: Prostate volume is measured on mpMRI (T2 planimetry or ellipsoid). TRUS volume may appear in the labs panel. Volume is needed to compute PSAD.

bx.note: Prior biopsy results (Gleason/ISUP grade, core count) live in the pathology report. Previous notes may contain narrative summaries of prior biopsy decisions.

comorbidity.note: Comorbidity status and life-expectancy context are in the comorbidities section. Prior notes may mention comorbidities discussed at earlier visits.
